Name: neorv32_sys_clock.enable_i
Description: Vector of enable channels that is OR-reduced to produce the internal enable 'en'; when deasserted the internal counter is held at zero and no clock-enable pulses are generated.
Justification: This external input gates the generation of all derived clock-enable pulses. If an integrator or attacker can force or keep this input deasserted (or manipulate it), peripherals and logic relying on the generated clk_en_o pulses can be starved of timing ticks, producing a denial-of-service of downstream functionality.

Name: neorv32_sys_clock.cnt
Description: 12-bit counter that increments while 'en' is active; individual bit rises are used to generate the single-cycle clk_en_o pulses for various divided clocks.
Justification: cnt enforces the watchdog’s liveness window; if it is stalled or cleared incorrectly the watchdog may never expire (hung systems persist), while premature or accelerated counting triggers spurious timeouts and resets—both conditions disrupt forward progress and service availability.
